response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web platforms, low-latency microservices, and developer tooling, but the Principal Performance Architect role requires deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the silicon level — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency async I/O work at Sociomantic and some C/C++ at National Instruments, but these do not approach the hardware co-design and AI accelerator performance domain this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering instincts through low-latency, high-throughput work handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and caching.
C/C++ experience at National Instruments working on measurement software used by hardware engineers, showing some proximity to hardware-adjacent tooling.
Long-tenured, breadth-oriented engineer with embedded systems exposure and a CS degree from a well-regarded institution (IIT Bombay).
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical requirement for this role is entirely absent from the resume.
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background, missing all three preferred qualifications.
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack and application software; there is no progression toward hardware-software co-design or AI accelerator performance analysis.

key_factors.factor: No GPU or DNN performance background
Low-latency systems work shows performance awareness
